role: Technical Lead – Senior Staff Cloud Systems Engineer
description: Strategic infrastructure leadership focused on multi-region architecture, networking optimization, and organizational change management. Collaborated directly with senior engineering leadership on Pinterest's infrastructure strategy.
achievements: Led Pinterest's Multi-Region and Multi-Account initiatives, collaborating directly with senior engineering leadership.
Architected Pinterest's Next-Gen Network Architecture, enhancing infrastructure stability by combining centralized connectivity with distributed control mechanisms.
Conducted comprehensive analysis on AZ balances and data transfer costs, transitioning from Transit Gateway to direct VPC peering, delivering multi-million dollar annual networking cost savings.
Successfully advocated for organizational adoption of a Multi-Account architecture, influencing Pinterest's long-term infrastructure strategy.
Supported major Data Engineering initiatives, including Spark on EKS, facilitating transition to a robust Multi-Account environment.
Founded and built the Pinterest Cloud Architecture team, defining core pillars, team charter, and strategic roadmaps.
Built DR tooling at a fraction of vendor cost; achieved order-of-magnitude improvement in recovery time.
Led EC2 provisioning at hyperscale—large-scale compute infrastructure and significant storage footprint.
Established Infrastructure as Code (IaC) standards and Terraform CI/CD pipelines, enhancing operational safety, consistency, and reducing deployment failures.
Directly mentored 12–14 engineers, significantly contributing to professional growth and retention.
Provided indirect mentorship across Security, Infrastructure Provisioning, and SRE teams.
Led cross-team educational workshops increasing internal engineering capability in cloud networking and IaC.
Deployed AWS Cloud WAN into production, delivering cross-region connectivity as the backbone for regional expansion.
Built comprehensive Terraform module library for Cloud WAN with zero-downtime migration support across egress patterns.
